meta.purpose: PR Split — Delete ALL 'osm mcp*' commands (mcp, mcp-instance, mcp-make, mcp-parent), purge MCPInstance infrastructure from claudemux, fix bugs, enhance TUI, add integration tests. mcpcallback is the sole IPC path. Flat sequential task list; NO priorities; NO estimates; ALL tasks MUST be completed.
meta.source: DIRECTIVE.txt, config.mk Hana directive, scratch/NOTES.md, codebase survey 2026-03-01
meta.maintenance: This file is the single source of truth. Update task status immediately after every sub-task. Deviations must be logged within the plan. Read this file and WIP.md at session start before any work.

globalAlerts: CROSS-PLATFORM BUILD: The build MUST pass on Linux (ubuntu-latest), Windows (windows-latest), and macOS (macos-latest). CI matrix at .github/workflows/ci.yml. No exceptions.
BUILD SYSTEM: GNU Make is the build system. `make` runs build+lint+test. Use `gmake` on macOS if system make is BSD. All verification runs through Make targets.
DEADCODE POLICY: NEVER add ignores to .deadcodeignore. If deadcode reports an unused function, wire it up or delete it. Ignoring dead code is forbidden per AGENTS.md.

HANA DIRECTIVE (config.mk line 1): The ENTIRE existing 'osm mcp*' command family is SLOP. mcp.go, mcp_instance.go, mcp_make.go, mcp_parent.go, and ALL associated test files MUST be DELETED. MCPInstance infrastructure in claudemux MUST be DELETED. mcpcallback is the sole IPC mechanism.
osm:mcpcallback is the SOLE IPC mechanism. All file-polling (pollForFile) and file-writing (mcpWriteResultFile) was legacy slop already purged in prior sessions.
mandatoryDirectives: Read blueprint.json and WIP.md at the start of every session before doing any work.
Update task status in blueprint.json immediately after completing each sub-task.
Run `make` (full suite: build + lint + test) after every phase boundary.
Subagents spawned for implementation work must NOT self-review or run the Rule of Two.

statusSection.goalState: The osm pr-split system uses EXCLUSIVELY osm:mcpcallback for IPC. The 'osm mcp', 'osm mcp-instance', 'osm mcp-make', and 'osm mcp-parent' commands are DELETED. mcp.go, mcp_instance.go, mcp_make.go, mcp_parent.go, and all associated files are GONE. MCPInstance infrastructure in claudemux (mcp_config.go, mcp_guard.go, wrapMCPInstance, newMCPInstance binding) is GONE. pollForFile is GONE. All validation lives in JS. All bugs from NOTES.md are fixed. TUI has streaming verification and per-branch status. Integration tests with real AI validate the spawn-switch-send-classify pipeline end-to-end. All three platforms are green.
statusSection.currentState: Build GREEN on macOS. Legacy file-polling PURGED from pr_split_script.js. mcpcallback is the sole IPC path. All tests converted from file-based to channel-injection via WatchForInit/InjectToolResult. pollForFile deleted. All old test infrastructure deleted. resolveConflictsWithClaude tests use mock mcpCallbackObj. TestIntegration_AutoSplitMockMCP migrated to mcpcallback IPC. Next: T1 (pre-flight verify), then T2-T14 (MCP deletion chain).

replanLog.trigger: Hana directive in config.mk line 1: entire osm mcp command family is slop, must be deleted
replanLog.summary: COMPLETE BLUEPRINT REWRITE. Pruned 44 completed tasks. Renumbered remaining and new tasks T1-T46. Added mcp_make.go and mcp_parent.go to deletion chain (survey missed these — removing registrations without deleting files would trigger deadcode failures). Added PTY reliability tasks. Expanded claudemux MCPInstance cleanup to cover instance.go MCP field, instance_test.go, and all mcp_* files.

sequentialTasks.description:
Run `make lint` which includes the deadcode checker. The massive deletion in T2-T11 may have orphaned functions, types, or imports. Fix ALL findings by either:
  (a) Deleting truly unused code, or

NEVER add entries to .deadcodeignore. The existing entries in .deadcodeignore (testutil, mouseharness, ctxutil, scripting, storage) are for cross-package test helpers and MUST remain.

sequentialTasks.task: T13: go mod tidy
sequentialTasks.description: Run `go mod tidy` to clean go.mod and go.sum. The MCP command deletions may have removed the last import of certain dependencies (e.g., the MCP SDK if mcp_make.go or mcp.go were the only consumers). Verify no diff after a second `go mod tidy` (idempotency check).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: `go mod tidy && go mod tidy` produces no diff. `git diff go.mod go.sum` shows only legitimate removals (no additions of unrelated deps). `make` still passes.

sequentialTasks.task: T14: Full build verification after MCP deletion chain
sequentialTasks.description: Run `make` on macOS — full build + lint + test. This is the critical phase boundary gate after the entire MCP deletion chain (T2-T13). Also re-run `make integration-test-prsplit-mcp` to confirm the mock integration test still passes with the deleted code removed.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: `make` exits 0. `make integration-test-prsplit-mcp` exits 0. Zero compilation errors. Zero test failures. Zero lint warnings.

sequentialTasks.task: T15: Fix verification skip bug — step() ignores allPassed:false
sequentialTasks.description:
Bug location: internal/command/pr_split_script.js.
Root cause: The step() function (line 2577) only checks `result.error` to determine failure. The 'Verify splits' step (line 3041) calls verifySplits() which can return `{error: null, allPassed: false}` when individual branches fail (line 1313). Since error is null, step() treats this as success, and the pipeline continues as if verification passed.
Fix: In the 'Verify splits' step callback (around line 3066), when `verifyObj.allPassed` is false and there are `realFailures`, set `error` to a descriptive string instead of null. E.g.: `return { error: realFailures.length + ' branch(es) failed verification', failures: realFailures, allPassed: false };`
Alternatively, fix step() itself to also check an `allPassed` field — but the simplest fix is at the step callback level.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: When verifySplits returns allPassed:false with failures, the step reports an error. The pipeline does NOT silently proceed past failed verification. TUI shows the failure. Existing tests updated to match new behavior.

sequentialTasks.task: T16: Fix sendToHandle null handle at 4 unguarded call sites
sequentialTasks.description:
Bug location: internal/command/pr_split_script.js.
The sendToHandle function (defined at line 2454) writes text to a Claude PTY handle. There are 4 call sites where claudeExecutor.handle could be null if the Claude process died or spawn failed:
  1. Line 1757: resolveConflictsWithClaude single-branch path
  2. Line 2815: classification prompt send in automatedSplit
  3. Line 3093: re-classify constraint prompt in automatedSplit
  4. Line 3323: conflict resolution prompt in automatedSplit
Fix: Before each sendToHandle call, check `if (!claudeExecutor || !claudeExecutor.handle)` and return an error object like `{ error: 'Claude process handle is null — process may have exited' }`. The sendToHandle function itself should also guard: `if (!handle) return { error: 'handle is null' };`
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All 4 call sites have null guards. sendToHandle(null, ...) returns {error: ...} instead of crashing. Tests validate null handle behavior.

sequentialTasks.task: T17: Write tests for verification skip and sendToHandle null guard fixes
sequentialTasks.description:
Add tests in internal/command/pr_split_test.go (or a new pr_split_bugfix_test.go):
  1. Test that verifySplits returning allPassed:false causes the pipeline step to report an error (validates T15 fix)
  2. Test that sendToHandle with a null handle returns an error object (validates T16 fix)
  3. Test that automatedSplit handles a dead Claude process gracefully (handle becomes null mid-pipeline)
All tests must run with -race flag and be fully self-contained (t.TempDir(), no host state mutation).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Both bug fixes have dedicated test coverage. `go test -race ./internal/command/... -run 'Test.*VerifySkip\|Test.*NullHandle'` passes. Tests are deterministic across runs.

sequentialTasks.task: T18: Add JS-side validateClassification function
sequentialTasks.description:
Add to internal/command/pr_split_script.js a function `validateClassification(categories, knownFiles)` that validates classification data returned by Claude via mcpcallback. This replaces validation that was in the deleted mcp.go handlers (Go-side ValidateClassificationRequest/Response in internal/builtin/claudemux/split_protocol.go lines 43-74).
Validation rules:
  1. categories must be a non-empty array
  2. Each category must have non-empty name, description, and files array
  3. No duplicate files across categories
  4. Each file path should exist in knownFiles (if provided) — warn but don't fail on unknown files
  5. Return {valid: true} or {valid: false, errors: [...]}
sequentialTasks.acceptance: validateClassification() exists in pr_split_script.js and is called in the classification receive step. Tests cover: valid input, empty array, duplicate files across categories, missing required fields, unknown file paths. Function is exported via globalThis.prSplit for testability.

sequentialTasks.task: T19: Add JS-side validateSplitPlan and validateResolution functions
sequentialTasks.description:
Add to internal/command/pr_split_script.js:
  1. `validateSplitPlan(stages)` — validates split plan data. Rules: non-empty array, each stage has non-empty name and files array, no duplicate files across stages, valid branch name format.
  2. `validateResolution(resolution)` — validates conflict resolution data. Rules: has at least one of patches/commands/preExistingFailure, patches (if present) are non-empty strings.
These replace Go-side ValidateSplitPlanRequest/ValidateSplitPlanProposal in split_protocol.go (lines 112-134).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Both functions exist, are called at their respective receive steps, and are exported via globalThis.prSplit. Tests cover valid and invalid inputs for each. Return shape: {valid: true} or {valid: false, errors: [...]}.

sequentialTasks.task: T20: Pipeline timeout and watchdog
sequentialTasks.description:
Per scratch/NOTES.md: Add configurable timeouts to the automatedSplit pipeline in pr_split_script.js:
  1. --pipeline-timeout (default 120 minutes) — overall pipeline timeout
  2. Per-step timeout (default 60 minutes) — individual step timeout in step() function
  3. No-progress watchdog (default 15 minutes) — if no TUI update/output for 15 min, log warning and optionally terminate
Timeouts should be configurable via config object passed to automatedSplit(). Use Date.now() polling since Goja doesn't have native setTimeout.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Pipeline terminates with clear error on timeout. Per-step timeout causes step to fail with 'timeout' error. Watchdog logs warning then terminates. All three timeout values are configurable. Tests validate timeout behavior with short timeouts.

sequentialTasks.task: T21: Heartbeat monitoring for spawned Claude process
sequentialTasks.description:
Register a heartbeat tool in the mcpcallback MCP server that Claude calls periodically. If no heartbeat received within configurable timeout (default 5 minutes), log a warning. After 2x timeout (10 min), terminate the Claude process.
Implementation: Add heartbeat tool registration in the mcpcallback setup within pr_split_script.js. Track lastHeartbeat timestamp. Check in the watchdog loop from T20.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Heartbeat tool is registered. Claude process termination occurs after 2x timeout with no heartbeat. Warning logged at 1x timeout. Configurable timeout. Tests validate with mock mcpcallback.

sequentialTasks.task: T22: Dependency-aware verification bailing
sequentialTasks.description:
Per scratch/NOTES.md: When a base branch fails verification, all dependent branches should be skipped with a reason. Currently verifySplits (line 1260) already has dependency tracking via `failedBranches` map and `split.dependencies` check (lines 1285-1296). Verify this works correctly and enhance:
  1. TUI should show a distinct icon (⊘) for dependency-skipped branches vs failed branches (✗)
  2. Skip reason should include which dependency failed
  3. Skipped branches should not count toward failure count for retry decisions
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Dependent branches skipped with clear reason. TUI shows ⊘ icon for skipped branches. Skip reason names the failed dependency. Tests validate the dependency chain: A fails → B (depends on A) is skipped.

sequentialTasks.task: T23: Fix misleading hash indicators in verification UI
sequentialTasks.description: Per scratch/NOTES.md: The verification TUI shows hash indicators that may be inaccurate. Audit the hash display code in the AutoSplitModel view rendering. Either make hashes accurate (show actual git tree hashes from verification) or remove them entirely if they add no value.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Hash indicators are either provably accurate (showing real tree hashes from git operations) or removed. No misleading information in the TUI.

sequentialTasks.task: T24: Verification target auto-discovery
sequentialTasks.description:
Default verification command should be `make` (the project standard). Auto-discover Makefile presence via `stat Makefile` or `stat makefile` or `stat GNUmakefile`. Allow override via pr-split config flag or the plan's verifyCommand field.
Currently verifySplits uses plan.verifyCommand — verify this is wired up correctly and add fallback to `make` when verifyCommand is empty.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Default verification is `make` when no verifyCommand specified. Override via config works. Makefile discovery is cross-platform. Tests validate auto-discovery and override.

sequentialTasks.task: T25: Fix pre-existing failure handling
sequentialTasks.description:
When a branch has a pre-existing test failure (failure exists on the base branch too), it should be marked as 'pre-existing' and not block the pipeline. The resolution tool already has a preExistingFailure field. Ensure:
  1. verifySplits detects pre-existing failures by verifying the source branch first
  2. Pre-existing failures are reported but don't count as real failures
  3. Pipeline continues past pre-existing failures
  4. TUI shows a distinct indicator for pre-existing failures
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Pre-existing failures are detected by comparing against source branch verification. Pipeline continues. TUI shows distinct indicator. Tests validate the flow with a pre-existing failing test.

sequentialTasks.task: T26: Per-branch verification scoping
sequentialTasks.description:
Instead of running full `make` for every branch, scope verification to affected packages when possible. Strategy:
  1. Analyze which files changed in each split branch
  2. Determine affected Go packages from changed file paths
  3. Run `go test ./affected/package/...` instead of full `make`
  4. Fallback to full `make` if scoping can't be determined or if non-Go files changed
This reduces verification time significantly for large projects.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Verification scoped to affected packages when possible. Fallback to full make works. Tests validate scoped and fallback paths. Time improvement measurable.

sequentialTasks.task: T27: Ensure TUI reflects quick verification runs
sequentialTasks.description:
The AutoSplitModel should handle sub-100ms Running→Done transitions gracefully. Currently, very fast steps may not render their completion state before the next step starts, making them appear to be skipped.
Fix: Ensure step completion is always rendered at least once, even for sub-100ms steps. The TUI should show ✓ for completed steps regardless of speed.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Sub-100ms steps show ✓ in the TUI. No steps appear 'skipped' due to rendering speed. Tests validate with mock fast steps.

sequentialTasks.task: T28: Add logging for prompt sending and IPC failures
sequentialTasks.description:
Add comprehensive logging throughout the pr_split_script.js pipeline:
  1. Log before/after every sendToHandle call with truncated prompt content
  2. Log mcpcallback waitFor timeouts with elapsed time and tool name
  3. Log connection failures with error details
  4. Log validation failures (from T18-T19) with specific validation errors
  5. Use log.printf (not console.log) for all logging
This enables post-mortem debugging of pipeline failures.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Failed IPC produces actionable log messages. All sendToHandle calls have before/after logging. All waitFor calls log timeout details. Logs are greppable for 'auto-split' prefix.

sequentialTasks.task: T29: PTY newline delivery reliability (EAGAIN handling)
sequentialTasks.description: Investigated EAGAIN handling: Go's runtime poller handles EAGAIN transparently via SetNonblock + epoll/kqueue. JS-side sendToHandle already has 3-retry EAGAIN logic as defence-in-depth. Added write timeout (SetWriteDeadline) to prevent indefinite blocking if child hangs: DefaultWriteTimeout=30s, configurable via SpawnConfig.WriteTimeout and JS writeTimeoutMs option. Negative value disables. 6 tests pass with -race.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: PTY writes handle EAGAIN with retry. Write timeout prevents infinite loops. Tests validate retry behavior with a simulated full PTY buffer (if feasible).

sequentialTasks.task: T30: Terminal mux re-render after panel toggle
sequentialTasks.description: Added flicker-free panel restore: RenderFullScreen() emits CUP+content+EL (clear-to-EOL) for every row instead of ESC[2J (erase display) + Render(). Removed the flash-to-black caused by clearing the screen before rewriting content. VTerm.RenderFullScreen() method added. termmux.go toggle path updated. 6 tests verify all-row emission, no screen clear, content preservation, cursor position, visibility, and idempotency.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Panel toggle is flicker-free. The virtual screen buffer correctly diffs old vs new state. Visual regression test or manual verification on macOS terminal.

sequentialTasks.task: T31: Investigate exec.execv streaming capability
sequentialTasks.description: COMPLETED. Analyzed exec module (sync-only exec/execv), Goja concurrency model (single-threaded runtime, ChainedPromise + goroutine pattern from osm:fetch), and PTY module patterns. Designed pull-based exec.spawn API: child.stdout.read() → Promise, child.stdin.write(), child.wait() → Promise. Uses pump goroutines per pipe → bounded channel → event loop Submit. Require() gains adapter param. Full design in WIP.md.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Analysis in WIP.md with: current exec API summary, Goja event loop constraints, proposed exec.spawn API design, concurrency safety strategy, recommendation for implementation approach.

sequentialTasks.task: T32: Implement exec.spawn streaming API
sequentialTasks.description:
Based on T31 design, implement exec.spawn(cmd, args, opts) in the Goja exec module. The API should:
  1. Start a subprocess and return a handle object
  2. Call opts.onStdout(line) for each stdout line (if provided)
  3. Call opts.onStderr(line) for each stderr line (if provided)
  4. Call opts.onExit(code) when process exits
  5. Handle object has kill() and pid properties
  6. Be safe with Goja's single-threaded runtime (use channel + event loop integration)
File: internal/builtin/exec/ (new or modified file).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: JS code can call exec.spawn and receive streaming output callbacks. Process kill works. Exit code is captured. No data races with -race flag. Cross-platform (Unix + Windows).

sequentialTasks.task: T33: Tests for streaming exec
sequentialTasks.description:
Write comprehensive tests for exec.spawn:
  1. Real-time stdout line-by-line output
  2. Stderr capture
  3. Process kill
  4. Exit code capture
  5. Concurrent spawns (multiple processes)
  6. Large output (buffering correctness)
  7. Process that writes mixed stdout/stderr
  8. Cross-platform behavior (line endings, etc.)
Tests in internal/builtin/exec/ test files.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All tests pass with -race on all three platforms. Tests use t.TempDir() and t.Setenv() for isolation. No flaky tests.

sequentialTasks.task: T34: AutoSplitModel streaming output support
sequentialTasks.description:
Add streaming output display to the AutoSplitModel TUI:
  1. Define new Bubble Tea message type: AutoSplitStepOutputMsg{Step string, Line string}
  2. Update AutoSplitModel to store output lines per step
  3. Update the view to render streaming output below the current step
  4. Limit displayed lines (e.g., last 10 lines) to prevent TUI overflow
  5. Auto-scroll to latest output
Files: internal/command/pr_split.go (or wherever AutoSplitModel is defined), autosplit TUI types.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Streaming output lines appear in the TUI during step execution. Output is truncated to a reasonable limit. Auto-scroll works. Model update is race-free.

sequentialTasks.task: T35: Wire streaming exec into verifySplits
sequentialTasks.description:
Replace the synchronous exec.execv call in verifySplit (the per-branch verification function) with exec.spawn. Route stdout/stderr lines through the outputFn callback, which should emit AutoSplitStepOutputMsg to the TUI.
File: internal/command/pr_split_script.js, verifySplit function around line 1260.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Verification output streams to TUI in real-time during `make` execution. User can see build progress as it happens. Existing verification logic (pass/fail, exit code, timeout) still works correctly.

sequentialTasks.task: T36: Per-branch verification status in TUI
sequentialTasks.description:
Display individual branch verification results with icons in the AutoSplitModel:
  1. ✓ for passed branches
  2. ✗ for failed branches (with exit code)
  3. ⊘ for skipped branches (dependency failure, from T22)
  4. ⟳ for in-progress branches
  5. ⚠ for pre-existing failure indicator (from T25)
Summary line: '3/5 passed, 1 failed, 1 skipped'. Branch icons rendered as indented sub-items under the verify step. branchSummaryLine() method computes counts.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Per-branch status visible in TUI with correct icons. Summary line shows accurate counts. Tests validate rendering for each status.

sequentialTasks.task: T37: Per-branch TUI events from verifySplits loop
sequentialTasks.description: Emitted branchStart/branchDone events from JS verifySplits loop via autoSplitTUI.branchStart(name) and autoSplitTUI.branchDone(name, passed, exitCode, elapsedMs, skipped, preExisting). Go bindings in pr_split.go delegate to SendBranchVerifyStart/SendBranchVerifyDone. Update handlers in autosplit.go process AutoSplitBranchVerifyStartMsg and AutoSplitBranchVerifyDoneMsg, setting BranchVerifyStatus. Skipped branches also emit branchDone with skipped=true. onBranchStart/onBranchDone callbacks passed through options parameter.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Distinct events emitted per branch. TUI model processes all three event types. Branch status updates in real-time during verification.

sequentialTasks.task: T38: Failed branch details view
sequentialTasks.description:
Added expandable details view for failed branches:
  1. j/k navigates branch cursor (▸ indicator)
  2. Enter on failed/pre-existing branch expands output in the bottom pane
  3. j/k scrolls output when expanded, Enter/Escape collapses
  4. renderBranchDetail shows header line + scrollable output
  5. Output stored per-branch via BranchVerifyOutputMsg (max 200 lines)
  6. branchOutput callback wired JS→Go→TUI
  7. Help bar shows context-sensitive hints
Files: autosplit.go (model, Update, View, renderBranchDetail, branchScrollUp/Down), pr_split.go (branchOutput binding), pr_split_script.js (onBranchOutput callback).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Enter expands failed branch output. Scrollable viewport works with keyboard and mouse. Escape/Enter collapses. Output is complete (not truncated). Tests validate expand/collapse behavior.

sequentialTasks.task: T39: Tests for TUI streaming and per-branch features
sequentialTasks.description:
23 new tests in autosplit_test.go covering all T36-T38 features:
  1. BranchVerifyStart sets running status
  2. BranchVerifyDone for passed/failed/skipped/pre-existing
  3. BranchVerifyOutput stores lines, respects max cap
  4. Start resets output

sequentialTasks.acceptance: All TUI features have test coverage. Tests pass with -race. No flaky tests. Coverage includes edge cases (empty output, many branches, rapid transitions).

sequentialTasks.task: T40: Pause/Resume for auto-split sessions
sequentialTasks.description:
Added Ctrl-P pause mechanism to AutoSplitModel TUI:
  1. `paused` bool field set by Ctrl-P key handler (only when not done)
  2. `Paused()` method with mutex protection for goroutine-safe access
  3. Go binding `paused` in pr_split.go autoSplitTUI object
  4. `isPaused()` JS function in pr_split_script.js (mirrors isCancelled pattern)
  5. Pause check in step() function — saves checkpoint via savePlan() before returning paused error
  6. Separator shows '⏸ Pausing… saving checkpoint' when paused
  7. Help bar shows 'ctrl+p pause' hint during pipeline execution
  8. 5 tests: CtrlP_SetsPaused, CtrlP_NoEffectWhenDone, PausedSeparator, PausedDoneSeparator, HelpBar_ShowsPause
Resume uses existing `osm pr-split auto --resume` path (reads saved plan from checkpoint).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Ctrl-P pauses pipeline. Resume continues from exact checkpoint. Works across terminal disconnect. State survives process restart. Tests validate pause/resume cycle.

sequentialTasks.description:
ALREADY EXISTS. Three real-Claude integration tests are gated behind skipIfNoClaude:

  2. TestIntegration_RealClaudeCode (pr_split_test.go:6349) — real Claude spawn with mcpcallback IPC
  3. TestIntegration_AutoSplitWithClaude (pr_split_test.go:6407) — full auto-split with real Claude agent
All run via `make integration-test-prsplit` with -claude-command flag. Skipped gracefully when -integration not set.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test exists, gated behind -integration flag. Runs via `make integration-test-prsplit`. Validates real AI response structure. Cleans up processes. Skipped gracefully when -integration not set.

integrationTestCommands: make integration-test-prsplit-mcp (all platforms, no real AI required)
make integration-test-prsplit (macOS only, requires real Claude)

finalEnforcementProtocol: Cross-platform build MUST pass on ALL platforms — no exceptions.
NEVER add to .deadcodeignore — wire up or delete.
No AI Slop — the osm mcp command family was slop and it was DELETED.
Rule of Two before any commit.
The blueprint is the single source of truth.
mcpcallback is the SOLE IPC mechanism — any code re-introducing file-polling is a regression.
